pattern: The number of complete rows is the largest k whose triangular number k(k+1)/2 does not exceed n, found by solving that quadratic or binary-searching the row count.
hints: Row i needs i coins, so completing k rows costs 1 + 2 + ... + k coins in total.
You want the largest k with k(k+1)/2 <= n; that is a monotonic condition you can search or invert.
Solve k(k+1)/2 <= n directly with the quadratic formula floor((sqrt(8n+1) - 1)/2), or binary-search k over [0, n] checking the triangular-number bound.

edge_inputs.description: first example, 5 coins fills two full rows
second example, 8 coins fills three full rows
single coin makes exactly one row
exact triangular number fills k rows with none left over
one short of a triangular number drops a row
large value near the 32-bit ceiling stresses precision
two coins complete one row with one left over

stress.description: large coin counts up to the 32-bit ceiling expose float-precision errors in the closed form
stress.note: wide range catches sqrt rounding bugs near triangular boundaries

stress.description: values placed exactly on triangular boundaries n=k(k+1)/2 and just below them
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 65535)
    tri = k * (k + 1) // 2
    return [rng.choice([tri, tri - 1, tri + 1])]
stress.note: boundary-targeted off-by-one stress for the row count
